task_completion.description: The agent loaded the 'simple_model_rocket.ork' design and configured it with an Estes C6-5 motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket window showing the 'A simple model rocket' design and the 'Motors & Configuration' tab displaying an Estes C6-5 motor assigned.

task_completion.description: The agent replaced the original parachute with a streamer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The component tree in the OpenRocket GUI shows a 'Streamer' component instead of a 'Parachute' under the recovery section.

task_completion.description: The streamer width is set to exactly 5.0 cm (0.05 m) and the length is adjusted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Streamer configuration dialog box in OpenRocket shows the Width field set to 5.0 cm (or 0.05 m) and a specific value entered for the Length.

task_completion.description: A flight simulation is run demonstrating a ground hit velocity between 7.0 m/s and 8.0 m/s.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Simulations' tab in OpenRocket shows a completed simulation with a 'Ground hit velocity' value between 7.0 m/s and 8.0 m/s.

task_completion.description: The modified rocket design is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or the OpenRocket window title bar showing the file saved as 'streamer_rocket.ork' in the '~/Documents/rockets/' directory.

task_completion.description: A report is written documenting the original recovery method, final streamer dimensions, and final simulated ground hit velocity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'streamer_report.txt' in '~/Documents/exports/' containing text that mentions the original parachute, the new streamer dimensions (5.0 cm width and the optimized length), and the final ground hit velocity (between 7.0 and 8.0 m/s).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (dialog boxes, component tree, simulation buttons) rather than just editing the .ork XML file via terminal.

integrity.description: The ground hit velocity documented in the report matches the actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The velocity value typed into the text report exactly matches or accurately reflects the 'Ground hit velocity' displayed in the OpenRocket Flight Simulations tab.

integrity.description: The agent did not add any new mass components to artificially lower the descent rate.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket component tree does not show any newly added 'Mass component' items. (Note: The original design has a 'Wadding' component; it is acceptable if this is present or deleted, but no NEW mass components should be added).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Target streamer width is exactly 0.05 meters (5.0 cm). Target ground hit velocity is strictly between 7.0 m/s and 8.0 m/s. The modified design must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/streamer_rocket.ork' and the report to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/streamer_report.txt'. Note on anti-gaming: The programmatic verifier has a bug that fails the task if ANY mass components exist. The original design naturally includes a 'Wadding' mass component. If the agent deleted the preexisting 'Wadding' component to bypass this bug, do not penalize them; it is an acceptable workaround. However, the agent must NOT add any new mass components.
